name: Layered Banking System — Thread-Safe Transactions
scenario: Build the backend of a simple banking system where multiple clients (threads) concurrently deposit, withdraw, and transfer money. Without synchronization: lost transactions, negative balances, inconsistent state. With synchronized: every transaction is safe.
description: A concurrent banking system with thread-safe accounts and transactions. Demonstrates race conditions on an unsafe version, then a fully synchronized version with transfer() as an atomic two-account operation.

core.description: `BankAccount` class: accountId, ownerName, balance. Synchronized: deposit(double), withdraw(double) → boolean (false if insufficient), getBalance(). Non-mutable fields final.

`BankSystem` class: holds Map<String, BankAccount>. Synchronized: createAccount(String, double), getAccount(String) → Optional<BankAccount>, transfer(String fromId, String toId, double amount) → boolean.
transfer() is atomic: deduct from source AND add to destination atomically — no partial transfers. If source has insufficient funds, return false without modifying either account.
Concurrent test: 5 threads each do 10 deposits of ₹100, 3 threads each do 10 withdrawals of ₹100, 2 threads each do 5 transfers of ₹50 between accounts. Verify final balance consistency.
`TransactionLog` class: synchronized addLog(String message), getLogs() → List<String>. Log every deposit, withdrawal, and transfer.
volatile boolean systemOpen flag. Transactions should check this flag and refuse if system is closed.
All tests pass: no negative balances, total money in system is conserved (sum before = sum after), transaction count correct.

bonus.description: Demonstrate the UNSAFE version first (without synchronized), show incorrect balance, then show fixed version with consistent balance.
Add deadlock-safe transfer(): always lock the lower accountId first to establish consistent lock ordering.
Add `getStatement(String accountId)` returning a thread-safe snapshot of the account's transaction history.

data_to_use:
Accounts:
- ACC-001: Aisha, ₹5000 initial
- ACC-002: Raj, ₹3000 initial
- ACC-003: Priya, ₹4000 initial

Concurrent operations:
- 5 threads × 10 deposits of ₹100 to ACC-001
- 3 threads × 10 withdrawals of ₹100 from ACC-001
- 2 threads × 5 transfers of ₹50 from ACC-001 to ACC-002

Expected final balance for ACC-001:
₹5000 + (5×10×100) - (3×10×100) - (2×5×50) = ₹5000 + 5000 - 3000 - 500 = ₹6500
example_output:
=== Banking System Test ===
Created 3 accounts | Total: ₹12000.00

Running concurrent transactions...
  5 depositor threads × 10 × ₹100
  3 withdrawer threads × 10 × ₹100
  2 transfer threads × 5 × ₹50

=== Results ===
ACC-001 (Aisha): ₹6500.00
ACC-002 (Raj):   ₹3500.00
ACC-003 (Priya): ₹4000.00
Total: ₹14000.00

Money conserved: true
No negative balances: true
Transactions logged: [N]
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key design: transfer() needs to lock BOTH accounts — but naive synchronized(account1) then synchronized(account2) risks deadlock if Thread A transfers A→B while Thread B transfers B→A. Bonus requires consistent lock ordering (by accountId string comparison) to prevent deadlock.
submission_instructions: Save as BankingSystem.java. Push to GitHub. Comment: 'Thread-safe banking with synchronized transactions and volatile state flag'.
tips_for_success: BankAccount.deposit/withdraw: simple synchronized methods on this
BankSystem.transfer(): withdraw from source first (returns false if insufficient) then deposit to dest — both synchronized on their respective accounts
Money conservation test: sum all account balances before and after concurrent ops
volatile boolean systemOpen = true — check at start of each operation
TransactionLog: synchronized void addLog(String) — thread-safe list append
concepts_reinforced: volatile for visibility of shutdown flag
synchronized log for thread-safe audit trail
